title: Five-level safety taxonomy (safe/unchecked/trusted/extern/unsafe) lacks clear usage rules
description: The verification spec defines five safety levels: default (safe), unchecked, trusted, extern, and unsafe. Each has distinct semantics and audit implications. The documentation does not define the decision rule for which level to use in a given situation — it provides examples but no formal distinction. A programmer wrapping a C library must understand all five and their intended interaction to know which to apply where. unchecked relaxes aliasing checks; trusted names an assertion; extern marks FFI boundaries; unsafe enables arbitrary operations — but the boundaries between these are not formally specified. Add a decision table or flowchart explaining which level to use and why.
